dataflow.description: Spatial density + zip-code aggregation in Vega-Lite, fanned out via DATA_POOL (Chicago green roofs).

nodes.content:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv("docs/examples/data/10-green_roofs.csv")
return df

import pandas as pd

df = arg
# Fill numeric NaN with 0; pandas 3.0 raises TypeError when filling string
# columns with an int, so scope the fill to numeric dtypes and leave string
# nulls untouched.
numeric_cols = df.select_dtypes(include='number').columns
df = df.copy()
df[numeric_cols] = df[numeric_cols].fillna(0)

return df

import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd
from shapely.geometry import Point

# Read the green-roofs CSV and project to a GeoDataFrame in WGS84.
green_roofs_df = pd.read_csv('docs/examples/data/10-green_roofs.csv')
geometry = [Point(xy) for xy in zip(green_roofs_df['LONGITUDE'], green_roofs_df['LATITUDE'])]
green_roofs_df = gpd.GeoDataFrame(green_roofs_df, geometry=geometry, crs=4326)

# Spatially attach the Chicago zip-code polygon to each rooftop.
chicago = gpd.read_file('docs/examples/data/chicago.geojson')
joined = gpd.sjoin(green_roofs_df, chicago, predicate='within')

# Keep only the columns downstream views actually use, and drop geometry so the
# DATA_POOL serializes a plain DataFrame instead of the full GeoDataFrame.
return pd.DataFrame(joined[['LONGITUDE', 'LATITUDE', 'VEGETATED_SQFT', 'TOTAL_ROOF_SQFT', 'zip']])

joined = arg

top_10_largest = (joined.groupby('zip')['VEGETATED_SQFT']
    .sum()
    .reset_index()
    .sort_values(by='VEGETATED_SQFT', ascending=False)
    .head(10))

return top_10_largest

dataflow.name: Vega-Lite spatial density
